# Key Academic Papers on Cybersecurity D: # Sources: Publisher websites; CrossRef; Google Scholar; literature review compiled July 2026

and Governance: Risk Management, Firm Reputation, and the Impact of Successful Cyberattacks on Target Firms
column8: Successful cyberattacks decrease shareholder wealth by ~1.09% (three-day window); firms increase risk management post-breach; intra-industry contagion effects
Text-based cybersecurity risk measure predicts cyberattacks; high-exposure firms earn 8.3% annual risk premium

and Governance: Cybersecurity Risk and Bank Loan Contracting
column7: Breach disclosures increase loan spreads by ~30 bps on average

and Governance: Is Cybersecurity Risk Factor Disclosure Informative? Evidence from Disclosures Following a Data Breach
column8: Detailed cybersecurity risk factor disclosures reduce information asymmetry

and Governance: Do Firms Underreport Information on Cyber-Attacks? Evidence from Capital Markets
column8: Evidence that firms underreport cyber incidents prior to mandatory disclosure regimes

column4: International Journal of Accounting Information Systems
column8: Disclosure length increases linearly with time; larger firms provide more extensive cybersecurity risk disclosures

and Governance: Cybersecurity Risk and the Cost of Equity Capital
column4: Journal of Accounting and Public Policy
column8: Higher cybersecurity risk associated with higher cost of equity; moderated by board oversight quality

and Governance: Do Banks Price Firms' Data Breaches?
column8: Banks price data breach risk; passage of DBN laws increased breach-related costs for lenders

and Governance: Disclosure of Cybersecurity Investments and the Cost of Capital
column7: Digital cybersecurity investments show stronger negative association with cost of equity than cost of debt

and Governance: The Effect of Data Breaches on Company Performance
column4: International Journal of Accounting and Information Management
column8: Data breaches reduce ROA; no significant stock market reaction at quarterly level

column4: Auditing: A Journal of Practice and Theory
column8: Higher cybersecurity risk linked to higher audit fees and longer audit delays

and Governance: The Impact of Data Breach Announcements on Shareholder Wealth in the Banking Industry
column4: Journal of the Association for Information Systems
column8: Data breach announcements produce cumulative abnormal returns of -2% to -5%

and Governance: Empowering Women to Lead Cybersecurity: The Effect of Female Executives on Disclosure Sentiment
column4: International Journal of Finance and Economics
column7: Gender-diverse boards foster stronger cybersecurity disclosure practices
